test-frontend.note: 80 tests passed across 3 test files (utils, api, stores/tokenlist)

build-frontend.note: Production build succeeded (304.82 kB JS, 16.04 kB CSS)

typecheck-backend.note: No issues found in 12 source files

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Update Step 7 to use the port declared in services.yaml (5199) rather than the hardcoded 5173. The step currently reads 'Start the dev server: cd frontend && npm run dev (port 5173)' but services.yaml vite-dev.port is 5199 and workers consistently used 5199. Either update the port literal or instruct workers to read the port from services.yaml.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: All 4 reviewers (token-swap-on-conflict, remove-duplicate-token-icon, add-favicon, increase-api-timeout) flagged the port mismatch. Every worker transcript showed port 5199 being used while the skill text says 5173. This is a systemic issue across every worker in the milestone.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Clarify when TDD (Step 4 'Write Tests First') is applicable. Component-level event handlers (e.g., handleFromSelect in QuoteForm.svelte) cannot be unit-tested without a DOM harness, which is not configured in this project. The skill should distinguish between (a) pure utility functions and store logic — where TDD is feasible and required — and (b) component event handlers — where testing the handler directly isn't viable and testing the underlying store layer is the correct approach.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Feature 'token-swap-on-conflict': worker ran tests (all passing) before any edits, then implemented feature and tests together. Reviewer noted the TDD red-phase step was skipped because it's not feasible for component-level swap logic. The added tests correctly covered the store layer (saveCustomPair/getEffectivePair) rather than the component handler.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add an exception clause to Step 6 validators: svelte-check may be omitted for changes that only affect numeric constant values (no type changes). The current wording 'All must pass' with no exceptions caused two workers to record deliberate deviations for safe, trivial changes.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Features 'increase-api-timeout' and 'remove-duplicate-token-icon' both recorded skillFeedback.followedProcedure=false due to skipping svelte-check. In both cases the deviation was safe and well-reasoned (numeric constant change, no TypeScript type impact). The skill's zero-tolerance wording creates unnecessary deviation noise.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add a clarification that UI feature verification must use agent-browser (not mock-user). The mock-user droid simulates user observations via structured prompts but does not actually launch or render the app — it cannot detect real DOM or visual bugs. For UI features, agent-browser with an actual browser session against the running dev server is required.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Feature 'token-swap-on-conflict': worker used 'subagent_type: mock-user' for interactive verification. The fabricated port 5201 in the handoff (neither 5173 nor 5199) indicates the mock-user generated plausible-sounding but unverifiable observations. The validation contract (VAL-SWAP-001 through VAL-CROSS-001) explicitly states 'Evidence: agent-browser...' for all swap assertions.
